name: Airbyte CDK
what_it_does: Python SDK for building custom Airbyte connectors (sources and destinations). Provides base classes (AbstractSource, HttpStream, IncrementalMixin) that handle HTTP pagination, state management, schema inference, and retry logic. Custom connectors built with CDK run in Docker containers and integrate with Airbyte's orchestration layer. Used to connect custom APIs or proprietary data sources to Airbyte's connector catalog.
use_cases: Build custom Airbyte source connectors for internal APIs or proprietary data sources not in Airbyte's catalog
Create Airbyte destination connectors for custom data warehouses or streaming services
Implement incremental sync (cursor-based) for custom data sources to reduce API calls in recurring syncs
Publish custom connectors to Airbyte's open-source connector registry for community use
Build agent data ingestion pipelines where agent outputs need to flow into data warehouses via Airbyte
not_for: Teams without Airbyte infrastructure — CDK connectors only run within Airbyte's orchestration system
One-time data migrations — CDK is for recurring sync connectors; use dbt or custom scripts for one-time migrations
Real-time streaming — Airbyte is batch-oriented; use Kafka or Flink for real-time streaming
best_when: You need to build a reusable connector for a data source that isn't in Airbyte's catalog and want to use Airbyte's orchestration, monitoring, and scheduling infrastructure.
avoid_when: You don't use Airbyte, need one-time data extracts, or need real-time streaming — CDK connectors are batch-sync focused.

auth.notes: SDK library. Source/destination auth is implemented within the connector using Airbyte's spec/config pattern.

pricing.paid_tiers: None for CDK. Airbyte Cloud from $40/month for managed connectors.
pricing.notes: MIT license. Part of the Airbyte open source project.

performance.rate_limits: No CDK rate limits. Connector performance depends on source API limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CDK provides structured error types (AirbyteError) for sync failures. Connector logs surface in Airbyte's UI. Debugging requires running the connector locally with the Airbyte connector runner.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Incremental sync state is managed by CDK's state management — resumable syncs with cursor-based pagination.
known_agent_gotchas: CDK connectors run in Docker containers — local testing requires Docker and the Airbyte connector runner; development feedback loop is slower than pure Python testing
Schema inference vs. explicit schema definition — HttpStream can infer JSON schema but inferred schemas may change unexpectedly; explicit catalog specs are more reliable
Incremental sync requires implementing get_updated_state() — incorrect state management causes either duplicate data or missed records on subsequent syncs
HTTP rate limiting must be implemented in the connector — CDK's HttpStream handles retry but the rate limit strategy must match the source API's limits
Connector spec (config JSON schema) must match exactly what the source expects — mismatches cause authentication failures that are hard to debug without direct API access
CDK versions update frequently with deprecation cycles — pin CDK version and test upgrade paths; some CDK releases have breaking changes to base class APIs

security_score_components.security_notes: Credentials handled via Airbyte's secret management. Connectors run in isolated Docker containers. Source credentials never leave the connector's execution context.
